title: fraxinus excelsior f. diversifolia
intro: Fraxinus excelsior f. diversifolia, also known as the Ash-leaved Ash, is a rare variant of the European Ash (Fraxinus excelsior) tree. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has been used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and lower fever. The bark of Fraxinus excelsior f. diversifolia has been traditionally used to support heart health, alleviate rheumatism, and provide relief from digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herbal preparation of the bark is typically made into a tea or tincture and is also used in herbal remedies for its health benefits, including reducing stress and promoting overall well-being.
